headline: Bing Bong Doodlebug Sells 'Invisible Art' For $10,000, Calls It 'Commentary On Commerce'
summary: Buyers received an empty frame and a certificate saying 'imagine something beautiful.'
fullBody: Bing Bong Doodlebug has sold 47 pieces of 'invisible art' for $10,000 each. 'It's about what you don't see,' Doodlebug explained. Each buyer received an empty frame and a certificate reading 'Imagine something beautiful. Or don't. Art is subjective.' Critics call it a scam. Doodlebug calls it 'post-postmodernism.' The art has been featured in 'The Museum of Nothing,' which is also an empty room.
